enriched_points.expanded_content: The finite difference method approximates continuous equations by discretizing variables into grid points, facilitating numerical solutions of differential equations governing climate dynamics.
Widely utilized in climate models, finite difference methods help simulate atmospheric conditions, enabling predictions on weather patterns and long-term climate changes.
Advantages include simplicity and computational efficiency, while limitations involve challenges in handling complex geometries and potential numerical instability in certain configurations.
